title: At 42.8°C, Delhi logs hottest day of the year amid heatwave; relief likely next week - Moneycontrol.com
search_term: ("mercury soars" OR "mercury crosses" OR "temperature soars" OR "record temperature" OR "all-time high" OR "degrees Celsius" OR "maximum temperature" OR "hottest day" OR "temperature crosses 40" OR "temperature crosses 45" OR "temperature likely to rise") Haryana
full_text:
A slight respite is expected in the coming days, with the weather department forecasting light rain, thunderstorms and gusty winds between Monday and Wednesday.
Delhi sees its hottest day of the year - Delhi recorded its highest temperature of 42.8°C this year
- Heatwave conditions persisted for three consecutive days
- Air quality remained poor with AQI at 243
Did our AI summary help?
The national capital Delhi experienced its highest temperature of the year on Saturday, with heatwave conditions reported across several areas, according to the India Meteorological Department.
A yellow alert remained in place for the day, with a similar warning issued for Sunday amid continued concerns of isolated heatwave conditions.
At the Safdarjung observatory, which serves as the city’s primary weather station, the maximum temperature reached 42.8 degrees Celsius.
This was recorded at 5.1 degrees above the seasonal average and nearly one degree higher than the previous day, meeting the criteria for a heatwave.
It is also the highest temperature noted in the city since April 28, 2022, when it had touched 43.5 degrees Celsius. The minimum temperature at Safdarjung stood at 25.2 degrees Celsius, 2.5 degrees above normal.
Other monitoring stations also indicated intense heat across the capital. Lodhi Road recorded a maximum of 42.6 degrees Celsius, while Ridge saw temperatures climb to 44.5 degrees. Ayanagar registered 43.2 degrees Celsius, all significantly above normal levels.
Clarifying the duration of the current spell, IMD officials said the heatwave conditions had been ongoing for multiple days.
"On Thursday, one station, Rohtak to be specific, of Haryana and Ridge of Delhi, had satisfied heatwave criteria, thus making it the first day. Saturday was the third consecutive day of heat waves," they added.
A slight respite is expected in the coming days, with the weather department forecasting light rain, thunderstorms and gusty winds between Monday and Wednesday.
Maximum temperatures are likely to drop to between 38 and 40 degrees Celsius by the end of next week, while minimum temperatures may remain in the range of 25 to 27 degrees Celsius.
Meanwhile, air quality levels remained a concern. The 24-hour average Air Quality Index recorded by the Central Pollution Control Board stood at 243, placing it in the poor category.
This marked a rise from Friday’s reading of 226. According to CPCB standards, an AQI between 201 and 300 falls under the poor bracket.
Forecasts from the Air Quality Early Warning System suggest that pollution levels may remain in the poor range over the weekend before improving to moderate levels next week.

title: पंजाब और हरियाणा में भीषण गर्मी का प्रकोप जारी - ThePrint Hindi
search_term: (लू OR "लू चलना" OR "भीषण गर्मी" OR "प्रचंड गर्मी" OR "कड़ी धूप" OR "तेज धूप" OR "गर्म हवा" OR "उष्ण लहर" OR "ताप लहर" OR "हीट वेव" OR "झुलसाने वाली गर्मी" OR "तपती गर्मी" OR "भयंकर गर्मी" OR "गर्मी का कहर" OR "गर्मी का प्रकोप" OR "अत्यधिक गर्मी" OR मौसम OR "मौसम अपडेट" OR "मौसम का हाल" OR "मौसम पूर्वानुमान" OR "मौसम अलर्ट" OR आईएमडी OR "आईएमडी पूर्वानुमान" OR "आईएमडी अलर्ट" OR "तापमान में बढ़ोतरी" OR "गर्मी से राहत" OR "गर्मी से निजात") Haryana
full_text:
चंडीगढ़, 25 अप्रैल (भाषा) हरियाणा और पंजाब में शनिवार को भी भीषण गर्मी का प्रकोप जारी रहा। दोनों राज्यों के क्रमश: रोहतक और फरीदकोट में सबसे अधिक तापमान दर्ज किया। मौसम विभाग ने इसकी जानकारी दी।
विभाग के अनुसार, दोनों राज्यों में अधिकतम तापमान सामान्य से छह डिग्री तक अधिक दर्ज किया गया।
इसने बताया कि पंजाब के फरीदकोट में दिन का तापमान 45.2 डिग्री सेल्सियस दर्ज किया गया।
मौसम विभाग के अनुसार, हरियाणा के रोहतक में पारा 44.6 डिग्री सेल्सियस पर स्थिर रहा।
हरियाणा के अंबाला में अधिकतम तापमान 41.9 डिग्री सेल्सियस, हिसार में 43 डिग्री, करनाल में 42.8 डिग्री, नारनौल में 43.5 डिग्री, भिवानी में 42.5 डिग्री, सिरसा में 43.4 डिग्री और गुरुग्राम में अधिकतम तापमान 40.9 डिग्री सेल्सियस दर्ज किया गया।
पंजाब के अमृतसर में अधिकतम तापमान 40.3 डिग्री सेल्सियस, लुधियाना में 42.2 डिग्री सेल्सियस और पटियाला में 42.5 डिग्री सेल्सियस दर्ज किया गया।
हरियाणा और पंजाब की साझा राजधानी चंडीगढ़ में भी भीषण गर्मी का प्रकोप जारी रहा तथा अधिकतम तापमान 41 डिग्री सेल्सियस रहा।
भाषा जितेंद्र रंजन
रंजन
